commit.message:
Fix dashboard 404s: use rewrite rules instead of endpoint (v1.0.3)

The endpoint approach generated URLs like /dashboard/products/ but
WordPress expected /dashboard/tcg-section/products/. Replaced with
custom rewrite rules matching /dashboard/{section}/ directly.

Also added automatic flush of rewrite rules on version change.

commit.message:
Mount QZ cert into nginx container

The nginx image copies public/ at build time, so the cert baked in
is stale. Mount the host cert file so nginx serves the correct one.

commit.message:
Fix Python codegen for chained position references

The ActionStatementsBlockGenerator only used the first element of
position reference chains, ignoring subsequent elements. Now it
generates dimension_point.get_position() calls for each chain element
after the first, producing properly-wrapped Python code.

fix: remove duplicate DemoAppRegistry bean registration

feat: edit HM pencil everywhere + interviewer calendar availability grid

Edit Hiring Manager:
- New shared EditHMModal component (name, email, Slack handle) that
  PATCHes /api/hiring-requests/[id] — no API changes needed, the
  existing route already accepts any fields via spread
- Pencil icon appears on hover next to the HM in three places:
  • Jobs list table ("Hiring Manager" column) — updates the row inline
  • Hiring Request detail page — overview card, updates displayed fields
  • Schedule Interview modal HM banner — also re-fills the interviewer
    name/email fields and triggers availability refresh

Interviewer Calendar Availability Grid:
- When Google Calendar is connected AND an interviewer email is entered,
  a Mon–Fri availability grid appears below the email input
- Calls GET /api/google/availability (existing endpoint) with a 600ms
  debounce; re-fetches on week navigation or email change
- 8 AM–6 PM, 30-min slots; busy = red, free = green (clickable),
  selected = blue pill — clicking any free slot auto-fills Date + Time
- Week ‹ / › navigation to browse ahead; skeleton shimmer while loading
- Graceful "Calendar not visible" banner for external/non-Workspace emails
- "Use HM" quick-fill button auto-populates email from HM record when
  the interviewer email field is empty

commit.message:
fix: Hallucination Guard 오탐 수정

citation_tracker:
- 이모지+볼드 섹션 헤더(📌 **...**) UNSUPPORTED 오탐 제거
- 면책 문구(⚠️), 모순 검증 결과 줄 필터링 추가

guard:
- NONE_DECLARED를 ungrounded로 잘못 집계하던 버그 수정
- _keyword_check 제거: paraphrase된 정상 분석이 UNGROUNDED 처리되는 오탐 제거
  (ID 존재 여부까지만 검증, 깊은 faithfulness 검증은 eval LLM judge에서 담당)

결과: 동일 분석 기준 FAIL(ungrounded=41%, unsupported=43%) → PASS(0%, 0%)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

causing the mcpServers key to always show 'gmail' regardless of which
tool was being managed.

commit.message:
Fix QZ cert mounts: copy files instead of symlinking

Symlinks to /opt/shared/qz/ break inside Docker containers since the
target path doesn't exist in the container filesystem. Mount the
individual cert files directly in docker-compose and copy (not
symlink) in the provisioning script.

commit.message:
Add [tcg_register] shortcode for vendor registration (v1.0.2)

- Register shortcode in TCG_Dashboard class
- Process form on template_redirect to handle redirects before output
- Template shows validation errors on failure
- Successful registration auto-logs in and redirects to dashboard
- Enqueues dashboard CSS for form styling

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: multi-canvas support — one design canvas per open .ui file

- Add CanvasRegistry with interior mutability (Rc<RefCell<Vec<...>>>)
  so all mutating methods take &self, eliminating re-entrant RefCell
  panics from synchronous GTK signals during notebook operations
- Replace single canvas: Canvas in AppState with canvas_reg: CanvasRegistry
  and design_nb: GtkNotebook (one tab per .ui file in the Design view)
- Add close (×) button to design canvas tabs styled with editor-tab-close
- Auto-open style.css alongside .ui files; auto-close when .ui closes
- Wire on_close callback on NotebookManager to remove canvas from registry
- Fix borrow ordering in remove() and clear_all(): drop borrow_mut before
  calling remove_page() so GTK signal handlers can safely re-borrow
- Simplify all file-open sites to use borrow() — on_switch handles all
  canvas/toolbox/outline setup when a tab becomes active
- Session restore clones notebook+cfg before dropping state borrow so
  open_file() can fire switch_page signals without conflicting borrows

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: audit expert SYSCOHADA — 30 corrections sur 6 modules (74 anomalies, 33 tests)

Corrections P0 (bloquant sécurité) :
- AF-I01: fix amortissement dégressif VNC=money(0) → cumulDepreciation
- AF-I02: prorata temporis 365→360 jours (année commerciale SYSCOHADA)
- AF-C01: exclure brouillons de l'analyse budgétaire
- AF-T01: soldes bancaires calculés dynamiquement (positionService.ts)
- AF-CL01: trigger prevent_write_on_closed_period (migration 7)
- AF-CL03: trigger prevent_exercice_reopen (migration 7)
- AF-CL04: trigger prevent_audit_modification append-only (migration 7)
- AF-ER02: contrôle croisé Résultat Bilan = Résultat CDR

Corrections P1 (critique fonctionnel) :
- AF-T05: getHistoricalRate() pour taux au jour de pièce
- AF-I04: bascule automatique dégressif→linéaire
- AF-I05: trigger dotation sur bien cédé (migration 6)
- AF-I06: cap VNC ≥ 0 + CHECK constraint SQL
- AF-I09: ajustement dernière annuité (bouclage Σ=VO)
- AF-C02: trigger ventilation analytique Σ=100% (migration 6)
- AF-C05: versionnement budgets (colonne version)
- AF-C07: tables analytiques SQL + RLS (migration 6)
- AF-T10: tables rapprochements + lignes persistées (migration 6)
- AF-CL02: RPC execute_annual_closure atomique (migration 7)
- AF-CL05: affectation résultat obligatoire avant finalisation
- AF-CL08: sync cumulDepreciation après chaque dotation
- AF-ER01: RPC generate_bilan + generate_cdr PostgreSQL (migration 7)
- AF-ER04: table etats_financiers_archives avec SHA-256 append-only
- AF-ER05: table declarations_fiscales avec RLS
- AF-I07: table categories_immobilisations paramétrables

Migrations Supabase :
- Migration 6: 5 triggers + 5 tables + RLS + indexes
- Migration 7: 3 triggers sécurité + 2 RPC + 3 tables + RLS

33 tests passent (dépréciation 14, rapprochement 8, états financiers 5, TAFIRE 6)

commit.message:
docs: log API attack defense and connection pool hardening (2026-03-13)

Update DevLog, EditionLog, TODO to record root cause analysis and fixes
for the connection pool exhaustion attack: rate limiting on all article
endpoints, page_size cap 100→20, pool_timeout 30s→5s, Railway IP fix,
uvicorn access log suppression.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

security: Upgrade Vite to ^6.2.4 to patch CVE-2025-30208 and CVE-2025-31125

Both CVEs affect Vite's dev server @fs file-serving mechanism via
path-traversal bypass. CVE-2025-31125 (CVSS 7.5) is in the CISA KEV
catalog (actively exploited). Lockfile now pins vite@6.4.1 which
patches both vulnerabilities. npm audit reports 0 vulnerabilities.

Fix fatal error on activation: load vendor role class before using it

The activation hook called TCG_Vendor_Role::create_role() without
requiring the class file first, causing a fatal error.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ui: full visual overhaul + fix 10 functional bugs

Visual overhaul:
- Warm dark minimal theme (Claude-like amber accent, neutral grays)
- ChatGPT-style layout: full-width assistant messages, right-aligned user bubbles
- Refined header with subtle toggle buttons and connection status
- Sidebar with date-grouped conversations and gear icon
- Settings modal with backdrop blur and click-outside-to-close
- Message fade-in animation and streaming cursor
- Comprehensive prose CSS for markdown rendering

Bug fixes:
- Fix CodeBlock $derived syntax (was $derived, now $derived.by)
- Fix streaming markdown (now parsed via marked + DOMPurify + rAF throttle)
- Fix code blocks not using highlight.js (new use:action post-processor)

commit.message:
feat: implement unified configuration system with config-file-only enforcement

ThunderLLAMA now reads configuration exclusively from thunderllama.conf,
ignoring command-line arguments and external environment variables to prevent
configuration loss across sessions.

Core Changes:
- Created common/config-parser.h: C++ configuration file parser (334 lines)
  - Reads key=value format with comment support
  - Expands $HOME and ~ environment variables
  - Sets internal environment variables (THUNDER_LMCACHE, LLAMA_PAGED_ATTENTION, etc.)
  - Populates common_params structure

- Modified tools/server/server.cpp:
  - Added #include "config-parser.h"
  - Calls thunderllama_config_load_and_apply() in main()
  - DISABLED common_params_parse() - command-line arguments now ignored

- Configuration File: thunderllama.conf (154 lines)
  - Single source of truth for all ThunderLLAMA configuration
  - 12 configuration sections (model, optimization, server, performance, etc.)
  - Includes performance benchmarks (54-120 tok/s)
  - Documents failed optimizations (Speculative Decoding)

Management Scripts:
- start-thunderllama.sh: Reads config, pre-flight checks, launches server (287 lines)
- stop-thunderllama.sh: Safe shutdown with PID/port/name fallback (73 lines)
- restart-thunderllama.sh: Convenience wrapper (25 lines)
- status-thunderllama.sh: Server status + LMCache statistics (86 lines)

Documentation:
- docs/configuration-system.md: Complete system documentation (580+ lines)
- README-CONFIGURATION.md: Quick reference guide (200+ lines)
- BUILD-THUNDERLLAMA.md: Recompilation instructions (250+ lines)
- CONFIGURATION-SUMMARY.md: Delivery summary
- README.md: Added configuration system section

Iron Rule Created:
- ~/.claude/rules/thunderllama-config-only.md: Configuration file enforcement rule
- ~/.claude/projects/.../memory/MEMORY.md: Quick reference updated

Benefits:
- Configuration persists across sessions (no more parameter loss)
- One-command startup instead of complex command lines
- Version control and audit trail via git
- Prevents context compression from losing startup parameters

Verification:
- Compilation successful (7.0 MB binary)
- Config loading confirmed: "ThunderLLAMA: loaded config from thunderllama.conf"
- All management scripts tested

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

If a migration hasn't been run on production (e.g. 011 for slack_bot_token
or 016 for google_oauth_access_token), the SELECT silently returns null data
causing both integrations to appear disconnected with no indication of why.

commit.message:
fix: harden API against connection pool exhaustion attacks

Root cause: attackers flooded GET /api/v1/articles?page_size=100 with no
rate limit, exhausting the SQLAlchemy pool (size=10+overflow=15) and
cascading into 500s for all users.

Changes:
- articles.py: add @limiter.limit("60/minute") on GET /articles, /headlines,
  /saved, /related; @limiter.limit("20/minute") on translate.
  Cap page_size max 100→20 on all list endpoints.
- limiter.py: replace get_remote_address with _get_real_ip that reads
  CF-Connecting-IP → X-Forwarded-For → client.host (Railway proxy-aware).
- database.py: pool_size 10→5, max_overflow 15→10, pool_timeout 30s→5s
  (fail fast instead of 30 s queue cascade), pool_recycle 1800→600.
- Dockerfile: add --no-access-log to uvicorn to stop 500+ logs/sec flood
  that was causing Railway to drop 2782+ critical error log lines.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add crosshair button to QuerySelector to navigate to query editor

[Midtown !2280] feat: Support --task flag on coworker call-in (#2026)

* feat: Support --task flag on coworker call-in

When `midtown coworker call-in --task <id>` is run, the spawned coworker
is immediately assigned to that task instead of waiting for daemon
dispatch. The handler validates the task exists and is not completed,
builds the task prompt, sets up the task worktree, and executes
RecordTaskAssignment + BindCoworkerToWorktree effects after spawn.

Changes:
- Add --task clap arg to CoworkerCommand::CallIn
- Pass task_id through CLI → client → RPC handler
- In handle_coworker_spawn: validate task, build task prompt, set up
  worktree, execute assignment effects on successful spawn
- Resolve channel from task when not explicitly provided

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address review feedback for --task flag on call-in

1. Reject InProgress tasks — prevents dual-assignment when a task is
   already being worked on by another coworker. Same guard as task.claim.

2. Update task file on disk after spawn — calls update_task_owner() and
   set_task_in_progress_for_repo() so the task transitions from pending
   to in_progress with the correct owner. Without this, the daemon would
   re-dispatch the task on the next tick.

3. Fix prompt composition — when --agent, --prompt, and --task are all
   provided, the user's --prompt is now preserved alongside the task
   prompt instead of being silently dropped.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Address remaining review feedback for --task call-in

- Fix plan section gap: extract build_plan_prompt_section_from_parts()
  so the RPC handler can read plan/execution-skill data directly from
  persistent state without collecting a full WorldSnapshot
- Add unit tests for task validation (completed, in-progress, not-found)
  and prompt composition (--prompt + --task, --agent + --task + --prompt)
- Add unit tests for build_plan_prompt_section_from_parts (empty, skill
  only, plan only, both, missing file)
- Update README.md with --task flag in call-in command docs
- Update docs/architecture.md with --task code path description

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: support AI-generated first message for Facebook lead ads

Make reportMessage optional in /webhook/audit-lead. When not provided,
the chatbot uses its own AI to generate a personalized first WhatsApp
message based on the lead's form data (name, business type, website,
qualification answers). Falls back to a static greeting on AI failure.

Also stores Facebook lead context (businessType, website, formAnswers)
in the conversation's extractedData for ongoing qualification.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix framework guide duplication: protected headings, dynamic max_tokens, search dedup

Three root causes of duplicate sections in framework_guide.md:

1. Protected headings bug in dedup.py — all instances of required headings
   were protected from removal, including duplicates. Now only the first
   occurrence is protected; duplicate instances are eligible for removal.

2. Hardcoded max_tokens=4096 — caused LLM output truncation, leading to
   restarted sections. Now derives from settings.implementation_max_tokens
   (25% of context window), matching project context's approach.

3. No web search content dedup — overlapping search results about the same
   framework were concatenated into the prompt, causing mirrored repetition.
   Added sentence-level overlap detection to drop redundant snippets.

Also removed the fixed "Maximum 4000 words" constraint from the system prompt,
which contradicted larger token budgets.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

[Midtown !2281] fix: Allow dispatch of PR-linked tasks with no active session (#2024)

* fix: Allow dispatch of PR-linked tasks when no active session exists

Tasks with open PRs but no active coworker session (e.g., pending tasks
created for existing PRs like "rebase and land PR #X") were blocked from
dispatch by is_task_pr_protected(). This created a catch-22 where nobody
could pick them up.

Add active_names awareness to is_task_pr_protected() so PR protection
only applies when the task's owner has an active session. Pending tasks
(no owner) and tasks whose owner went away are now dispatchable.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Update architecture.md for active_names check in pr_protected_tasks

Reflects the new session-liveness condition in is_task_pr_protected():
tasks without an active owner session are not PR-protected, allowing
dispatch of pending tasks linked to existing PRs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Update doc comments on is_task_pr_protected to include active_names check

The function and field doc comments listed 4 checks but omitted the new
step 2 (active session check), which is the core behavioral change. Now
matches the updated architecture.md description.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Move merged-PR check before active-owner bypass in is_task_pr_protected

Addresses review feedback: the early return for inactive owner was
bypassing the merged-PR protection, allowing tasks with merged PRs
to be re-dispatched during the window between PR merge and task
completion. Now merged-PR checks fire first, regardless of session
state, while only open-PR protection is gated on active sessions.

Adds regression test for merged-PR + inactive-owner scenario.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* docs: Update snapshot.rs doc comments to match new check order

Syncs the pr_protected_tasks doc comment with the restructured
is_task_pr_protected logic where merged-PR checks fire before the
active-owner bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Update stale-PR test to match merged-PR-first check order

The merged-PR guard now fires before the active-owner bypass in
is_task_pr_protected(), so a stale tasks_with_open_prs entry pointing
to a merged PR correctly protects the task for auto-completion rather
than allowing recovery. Update the test expectation to match.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

- Path B (simmering): personal escalation modal → cold war or moral victory

3 interview templates gating on coaching_beef_arc_active and coaching_beef_accused_public.

debug inspector) with orange color scheme distinct from coaching_overhaul amber.
